metadata.title:
CITIZENS


OUTSIDE

MAYOR AND COUNCIL TAKE
UP VIADUCT PLANS IN STAR
CHAMBER SESSION.

pages.raw_text:
Mayor Moore and the city coun.
ell this morning snubbed several
leading citizens who were in wait.
ing as bridge committee to be
taken into consultation on the
question of what shall be done
about a new structure at Monroe
st. While those people sat around
Mayor Moore announced an execu-
tive session.


The the citizens left in a huff,
making such remarks as Whoever
heard of public body in executive
session over a public problem"


Following the star chamber dis
cussion Fifth ward committee
composed of H. D. Merritt, J. Wet
eel and T. Peddicord, was admitted
and requested that negotiations be
entered into for the construction OF
a viaduct -on N. Division st.


Councilman w. G. Estep said that
he and a lot of others were ready
to enjoin the city from using any
of the 100000 bridge fund for this
work. There was considerable hot
argument over this and finally,
Councilman N. s. Pratt, seconded
by Councilman Lenard Funk, offer
ed the following resolution, which
was adopted:


Resolved, that the city engineer
make plans and take them up, to-
gether with the Fifth ward commit
tee, with representatives of the In
ternational railroad, o. R. & N. and
the Washington Water Power Co.,
and arrive at some definite plans
for viaduct"


1t was stated that those 3 cor
porations might be induced to stand
their share of the expense.
